blocks.content: Shirone and his companions rented a carriage outside the harbor and headed to a resort on the west side.
The beach, with its expansive white sands, was beautiful even from a distance, lined with numerous shops and accommodations built along the mountain like a hillside village.
Rian had rented a villa halfway up the mountain.
True to its noble exclusivity, the property was spacious enough for the four of them, and the view of the ocean was satisfying from every angle.
From the terrace, Shirone admired the sea.
The blue waves crashed onto the sandy beach, creating foam.
Watching people leisurely enjoying the beach made it easier to momentarily forget the unpleasant confrontation at the harbor.
After settling in and lighting the fireplace, they gathered in the living room to relax from the journey.
Having skipped proper meals since morning, they were all hungry.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: An idea seemed to strike her.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: How about we cook ourselves?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What? You know how to cook?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Of course, I do. Did you think swordsmen can't cook?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I was worried it might be some kind of military ration.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Don't be ridiculous! I can cook for real, you know?
Rian was genuinely taken aback, not having expected the boisterous Tess to be confident in her culinary skills.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: This was precisely the reaction Tess had hoped for, seeing it as an opportunity to show Rian a different side of her during this trip.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: I'll prepare the most delicious meal, so just bring the ingredients. What do you say?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hmm, should we? It does seem a bit much to go out on our first day. Shirone, what do you think?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm fine with it. Food always tastes better when you're outside, right?
Amy tentatively offered her help.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Should I... help with the cooking?
All eyes turned to Amy.
While her prickly nature might suggest otherwise, no one doubted her ability to cook.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess sensed Amy's underlying motive.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: 'Is it for Shirone? Despite appearances, she's quite endearing.'

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess was all for it, hoping this could be a chance for the two to reconcile.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Great. Let's have a cooking showdown! Rian versus Shirone! You guys get the ingredients, and we'll prepare to cook. Sound good?
With everyone in agreement, Shirone and Rian took the shopping list from the girls and headed to the market.
Once the men had left, the atmosphere softened.
As they washed dishes side by side, Tess, with a playful glint in her eye, asked Amy.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Is this your first time?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: First time for what?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: You know. Your first fight with Shirone? Despite the argument, you seemed flustered, it was obvious.
Amy quickly diverted her gaze to the dishes.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It wasn't a fight. And definitely not our first, nor a lovers' quarrel.
With a typical mage's triple denial, Tess burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Mages may be cunning and secretive, but in moments like these, they seemed rather adorable.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Still, you were quite thorough with the shopping list. Are you maybe feeling a bit... nervous around Shirone?
Flustered, Amy stopped washing the dishes to ponder.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Since it was just the two of them, there was no harm in being honest.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone sometimes feels... distant.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Distant? That's quite poetic.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy chuckled at her own description.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But it's true. It's like he becomes transparent. As if he could disappear at any moment. Maybe it's because he sees the world differently from others.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Differently? He doesn't have extra eyes somewhere, does he?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Maybe. Most people see the world from their perspective. But some can expand their view to include others' perspectives. Such people are often called paragons of virtue.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Hmm, if that's the case, I'm definitely not one. I can't understand others' thoughts at all.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Neither can I. But Shirone is different. He's kind, considerate, and I think his unique perspective is the biggest factor.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess smiled, having an 'aha' moment.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: So, you're upset because he didn't take your side against those thugs? Is that it?
Amy awkwardly scratched her cheek.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Well, maybe... But if he were just a simpleton, I wouldn't have thought much of it. Shirone is different. In a way, he has a perspective that even goes beyond that of a paragon of virtue.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Beyond that? What else is there besides that perspective?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Is it like an omniscient viewpoint?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Omniscient…?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yes. When Shirone starts seeing the world from an omniscient point of view, he becomes faint to us. He becomes different, not just from us, but from any other human. It's probably because he has such an extremely high perspective that even his own existence seems like just a dot.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy vividly remembered the moment Shirone had opened the Immortal Function without hesitation to save the entire school.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: How could he do such a thing? It was different from suicide. Sacrificing one's life for others was thousands of times harder than choosing death due to personal suffering.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: When Shirone becomes transparent… the boundaries between himself and others dissolve. He becomes just another human among others. What governs Shirone when he looks down upon the world from that point? I think it's balance. If the scale tips even slightly, Shirone would not hesitate to step on the opposite side to balance it, even if that scale represents death.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ah, come on. That's impossible. How could someone like that exist? That's literally a sage.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: But it's true. Because I'm one of the people who benefited from that balance. No, all the students at the magic academy were saved by Shirone.
Tess couldn't close her gaping mouth.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: A wistful emotion flickered across Amy's face.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I just thought that when Shirone stopped me, maybe I was just another small dot from his high viewpoint. Shirone is different from me, from anyone. That's why he's an exceptional mage, but sometimes it feels disappointing.
Tess gently shook Amy's shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Don't think like that. Shirone truly cares about you. That's why he asked you to come on this couple's trip, knowing well what it was. You're not just another person wandering the streets. You'll always be Amy Karmis.
Encouraged by Tess's sincere words, Amy smiled.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: However, her thoughts didn't change.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: How much could Shirone, who could coldly abandon even his own life, truly value the existence of others?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Amy, just tell Shirone. Ask him why he didn't take your side. Tell him you're so upset and frustrated that you can't sleep. Just talk to him about it.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy chuckled and shook her head.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone knows. He didn't argue because he understood. Don't worry too much. It'll be resolved soon. It's just awkward because it's the first time.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: But Amy ended the conversation by saying there was nothing left to discuss since they both understood each other.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Sigh, I can never understand the way mages think.
Shirone and Rian arrived at the alley of miscellaneous shops.
Since their shopping lists differed, wandering through the maze-like market would likely take until evening.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone thought it best to split up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's part ways here. Once we've bought everything, we can head back to the villa.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Are you sure? Can you carry everything yourself? It might be heavy.
Shirone looked incredulously at Rian.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's fine! I'll manage on my own, so just don't get lost. Remember, it's a cooking competition. The loser does the dishes.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Alright! I hope you enjoy doing the dishes with Amy. You'd better hope to lose if you want to make up with her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Make up? We didn't even fight.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Didn't you argue at the harbor? And now it's all awkward between you two?
Shirone stopped talking.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As before, being called a fool by Rian strangely made him feel incompetent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Anyway, I'm off to buy the vegetables first. See you at the villa.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I'll start with the meat. See you there!
Shirone watched Rian dash towards the butcher's shop, imagining the amount of meat he would likely purchase.
Checking the note Amy had given him, Shirone saw nothing particularly difficult to find on the list.
Though the variety of ingredients was greater than Rian's, he needed to move quickly to keep up with time.
After about half an hour of shopping and sightseeing—since the resort area had many unique and interesting shops—Shirone had nearly completed his list.
The commercial district was bustling with entertainment, including vending machines offering games and various street performances, indicating a continuous influx of tourists.
Indeed, the streets overflowed with people, their laughter painting a picture of freedom not found on the mainland.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow, there are so many interesting shops here. I should have fun here tomorrow.
With his shopping done, Shirone headed back to the villa.
Suddenly, a shop door burst open, and a woman stumbled out, falling to the ground.
Kyaa!
Kyaa?
